metadata.name: RULES (for Elementary Cellular Automata) #3
metadata.description:
An important element of generative art is emergence. The most famous algorithm for this is probably Cellular Automata (CA).

However, numerous generative artists have created works using CA, which has lost its visual freshness and is like chewing gum that has lost its taste.

Although it is such a classic algorithm, adding my own little ideas to it and finding a new "flavor" is the reason why I code.

Here, the subject is 1D Elementary Cellular Automata, the simplest of all cellular automata. 1D Elementary Cellular Automata has 256 different rules, which change the shapes produced. This work aims to create more complex shapes by randomly selecting some of those rules and placing multiple of them on the screen.

The on/off of the CA cell is often likened to the behavior of living creatures.As of 2022, in the country where I live, prices are rising, wages are not increasing, and COVID-19 has not yet ended. I created this work with the image of people at the mercy of these various rules.

---

Screen click: Change rules
S key: Save png
L key: Show/hide grid

metadata.name: Quantum ATLAS #5
metadata.description:
Quantum ATLAS

This artwork was inspired by high-energy physics — the branch of physics that deals with the study of the fundamental particles and forces that make up the universe. It seeks to understand the structure and behavior of matter at the smallest scales, exploring the most fundamental building blocks of nature and the interactions between them.

High-energy physics investigates fundamental particles, such as quarks, leptons, and bosons, which are the building blocks of atoms and matter as we know it. It aims to answer questions about their properties, masses, charges, and the forces that govern their interactions. This field of study relies on powerful particle accelerators, such as the Large Hadron Collider, to create high-energy collisions between particles and observe the resulting reactions.

Detectors play a crucial role in high-energy physics experiments. They are sophisticated instruments designed to detect, measure, and analyze the particles and their properties that are produced during high-energy collisions in particle accelerators. Detectors enable scientists to collect data and gain insights into the fundamental particles and forces that govern the universe

The ATLAS detector is a large particle detector, one of the four main detectors at the Large Hadron Collider. ATLAS stands for "A Toroidal LHC ApparatuS" and is designed to investigate various aspects of particle physics, including the search for new particles, the study of the Higgs boson, and the exploration of fundamental forces and particles. The detector is massive in size, measuring about 46 meters in length and 25 meters in height.

By analyzing the vast amount of data produced by the collisions, scientists can gain insights into the fundamental nature of matter, unravel the mysteries of the universe, and potentially make groundbreaking discoveries that contribute to our understanding of the fundamental laws of physics.

Quantum ATLAS is my artistic endeavor that seeks to emulate the infinite variety of forms that elementary particles can exhibit during collisions within detectors. From elegant spirals and swirling vortexes to branching networks and pulsating clusters, it unveils a visual tapestry of shapes and structures that echo the fundamental processes occurring at the quantum level. The intricate lines in the artwork simulate the paths of particles as they traverse the detector, leaving behind traces of their presence.

Please use fullscreen or download artwork in high resolution for a better viewing experience. Chrome is highly recommended for high res saving.

Made with love, math, physics, and p5js.


Controls in fullscreen:
[1] - save PNG  1880 x 1280
[2] - save PNG 3760 x 2560
[3] - save PNG 7520 x 5120
[4] - save PNG 15040 x 10240

On mobile:
[quick tap] - save PNG

metadata.name: Possible Ways #44
metadata.description:
This project is written in javascript without using any other libraries, and it is an endless real-time software animation.

The appearance of each token is initially determined by the hash, but the size and positions of its elements change randomly over time, creating an infinite performance.

While working on this project, I was inspired by the elusiveness of the moment, that cannot be saved or predicted. It can only be experienced.

metadata.name: glory hole$ #283
metadata.description:
our consciousness as humans can be distilled into a crudely fashioned hole at an abandoned dave & busters and the meditative state that is endured while sitting on a neglected toilet praying for the sound of disrupted waters. glory holes embody anonymity and pleasure for pleasure's sake. they are creations that cannot be destroyed, as they exist in the memories of anyone who uses or witnesses. many speculate that the carving of the hole is the purest form of human ingenuity and the desire to explore the unknown. formerly limited to the physical space. these glory holes transcend space and time. and exist in that same unknown that tempts us all. legalize ground beef. create nothing. consume everything.

HUGE thank you to @PureSpider for the layered PNG template, this dream would not have been realized without you friend.

metadata.name: Lines, just lines #11
metadata.description:
"Lines, just lines" is my reflection on the beauty of simplicity and perpetual motion. At first glance, they are just lines dancing on a digital canvas. Yet, their complex and ever-changing choreography reveals an unexpected depth. I have created a ballet of lines that move with fluid grace, influenced by invisible forces such as "attractors" and "repulsors." These elements introduce an unpredictable dynamic, creating hypnotic movements that mirror the ordered chaos of nature.

The apparent simplicity of the work is deceptive, hiding a complex universe that translates this chaos into hypnotic movements. This piece invites viewers to lose themselves in a constant flow, to meditate on the emerging patterns and ephemeral forms that appear and disappear. It is a celebration of minimalism, where repetition and subtle variation create a captivating visual experience.

In our world saturated with visual stimuli, "Lines, just lines" offers a moment of pause, an opportunity to contemplate pure movement and form in its raw state. It reminds us that sometimes, lines, just lines, can tell an infinite and fascinating story.

metadata.name: Kunza #14
metadata.description:
In 1949, the last known native speaker of Kunza died in the Atacama desert of Chile, leaving behind only fragments of a language that stretched back to when the Incans ruled the region. The textures, patterns, and colors in this project are not intended to be historically accurate: they tell a story. They are my attempt to reach into the past and imagine a vibrant history that is increasingly inaccessible.

Features include weave design, symbol layout, symbol style, and color palette.

After the image has fully rendered, pressing ‘w’, ‘r’, or ‘c’ will toggle different elements of the image, and pressing ‘s’ will download a PNG file.

p5.js

metadata.name: NAZCA #155
metadata.description:
Real time animation (WebGL)
Variable dimension
2022

Nazca is a group of living glyphs made in the soil of the GPU. There are 17 figures that get combined and mixed over three layers. The figures vary in complexity but are generally created with vertical and horizontal lines only. Figures have at least one seed, some even have a seed per path. A seed is an oscillating pixel that can be compared to a square wave oscillator of audio synthesis and is usually followed by a path or a set of paths. The purpose of the paths is to send the pixels along the lines to make the figures visible through movement and to create a reduction to pure animation.

👽 Kim Asendorf

metadata.name: Sap song. Part II #62
metadata.description:
This artwork corresponds to the 2nd part of the ‘Sap song’ series. It seeks to expand the concept from new visual and sound variants.

Audiovisual generative artwork that continues with the proposal of excessive flows of organic forms. In time, frenetic arpeggios and populations of colorful plants with spiral flowers are emerging, all organized in their own way. And the sap again sings, feeds and shows us a constructive desire for afforestation.

The work has 3 moments:
I. “Enredo coral”
II. “Recitativo libre”
III. “Coda hacia la raíz”

Original samplers of cello pizzicatos, marimba, music box, piano, and multiphonics of clarinet and flute.

[ s ] -> save 2160x2160px (in live view)
[ f ] -> fullscreen

attributes.name: instruments:
attributes.value: cello, marimba, music box and flute

metadata.name: Another Perspective #2 #85
metadata.description:
This work was made using reverse perspective projection.
Perspective projection is common in traditional Western painting and 3D computer graphics.
However, it is not always necessary to use it, as in this work.

Drag the screen to change the camera angle.

Made with Three.js

metadata.name: Brosser #233
metadata.description:
Exploring generative brush strokes to simulate real life paintings. 

- A selection of 19 different palettes
- 4 different drawing styles
- Randomized "color swap" between the primary and secondary colors in each palette

Several other factors determine the output of a piece, including angles, depth, brush length, and brush weight, among others.

metadata.description:

metadata.name: Hueswirls #19
metadata.description: glsl plasma shader with random seed via hash

metadata.name: fxBLOCK #123
metadata.description:
fxBLOCK is a collection of speculative architectures. 
Each minted BLOCK is constructed using a set of polyvalent parts in various deterministic tectonic configurations, lights, and moods. 

fx(hash) genesis Generative Token by @grasser_alex 2021

metadata.name: "Alphabetic ancestral signs" - (Matrix v3) #63
metadata.description:
The "Alphabetic Signs" series finds its roots in different places: in my physical work, in a study on letters and typefaces, but also in graffiti and street art.

In this project I was inspired by the symbols of the ancestors. 
Prehistoric signs found in caves and hieroglyphs from different eras, such as the Inca or the Sumerian ones.
I wrote the Javascript code to compose 31 different ancestral signs that were used to fill a pseudo-random matrix.

Each element is coded to adapt to different areas on the canvas, tightening, stretching and expanding.

This work uses p5.js library.

metadata.name: ecologías - C #13
metadata.description:
ecologías - C

square forms and straight angles are often used to represent artificial, man-made artifacts. As machines enter into our societies - our ecologies - we may think what would be like a society of machines only. What are portraits of said machines without us humans around? Will they want to seek for their individual character, much like we do? It will depend on their personalities, their will, their intent, their traumas... their versions of all of this. We shall need to understand what their motivations will be like - what the potential basis for their potential version of a potential conscience is and how it will evolve. Only through a good understanding of diversity can we reach a functioning system. Both for ecologies with machines as well as ecologies without.

ecologías-C is an entity that is part of a broader ecology. Released on occasion of the show "Unbound - Ecology in the Age of Machines" at Unit London. The data generated by this entity is read by a different piece minted on Ethereum, creating a link between Tezos and Ethereum, a metaphor for an ecology of blockchains.

it shares a common element with two other works released on fxhash: ecologías A and ecologías B, all of them contributing data to aforementioned pieces.

To know more, visit https://www.iillucid.com/ecologias

Marcelo Soria-Rodríguez, @msoriaro
CC BY-NC-SA 4.0, https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/

Built on p5.js, glsl.

append &pd=N (N a number) to the URL in live view to increase/decrease resolution
press s to save current image in current resolution
press z,x,g,b,n followed by +/- to increase/decrease, respectively, brightness, contrast, gamma, blur, noise

metadata.name: RGB Elementary Cellular Automaton #431
metadata.description:
Stephen Wolfram's Elementary Cellular Automaton where 3 different rules are drawn in the Red, Green and Blue color components of the image.

Rules are selected randomly among a pre-selection of 53 rules from which nice behaviors emerge.

Tokens can have a cell size in the [1; 20] range.

metadata.name: Ethereal Microcosm #243
metadata.description:
An exploration of algorithmic organisms in a contained environment.

This piece is about the simulation of microscopic patterns using a high number of even tinier agents all following the same rules. Different species evolve and interact together, resulting in stabilizing patterns out of their parametric configurations. A form of artificial life.

This work is based on the paper "Characteristics of pattern formation and evolution in approximations of physarum transport networks", written by Jeff Jones and popularized in the artistic field by Sage Jenson. Some features were added to increase the visual and behavioral complexity of the system. Agents are divided into different species, with their own set of rules and interacting with their own substrate. Substrates also interact with each other and agents will mutate in contact of some of them.

NFT License
Runs properly on Chrome 97.0.4692.99
GPU intensive

Press P to pause (double tab touch devices)
Press
